EXPERIMENTAL TREATMENT: Abrasion of the seed coat was applied to allow water imbibition; binary (yes/no)
ECOLOGICAL FUNCTION: Delay germination until seed coat is abraded by soil movement, fire, freeze-thaw, etc.
PREDICTION: Stronger signal for high-disturbance species (e.g. germinate after fire)

EXPERIMENTAL TREATMENT: Seeds underwent a period (> 4 weeks) of cold-wet stratification (< 6¤C); binary (yes/no)
ECOLOGICAL FUNCTION: Delay germination until a cold season is passed
PREDICTION: Stronger signal for cold-stress species (e.g. avoid autumn-winter germination)

EXPERIMENTAL TREATMENT: Weighted average of the diurnal temperature cycle, weighted by the duration of each phase; in ¤C
ECOLOGICAL FUNCTION: Delay germination until the proper time of the annual temperature cycle
PREDICTION: Stronger signal for cold-stress/low-disturbance species (e.g. summer germination in alpine meadows, mires)

EXPERIMENTAL TREATMENT: Different temperatures (>5¤C) were applied during the day and night, in diurnal cycles; binary (yes/no)
ECOLOGICAL FUNCTION: Delay germination unless close to soil surface, under open vegetation and/or a low water table
PREDICTION: Stronger signal for low-stress species (e.g. germinate in vegetation gaps)

EXPERIMENTAL TREATMENT: Seeds were exposed to light during some part of the diurnal cycle; binary (yes/no)
ECOLOGICAL FUNCTION: Delay germination unless close to soil surface and/or under open vegetation
PREDICTION: Stronger signal for low-stress species (e.g. germinate in vegetation gaps)
